raw_consumer_statement:
My card was used in XXXX, NJ, on XX/XX/XXXX for four fraudulent purchases totaling {$750.00} while I was at work on the other side of the state in XXXX, NJ. 
The fourth charge ( {$110.00} ) triggered a fraud warning, and I received a text message warning. I IMMEDIATELY called in to report the fraud and discovered the first 3 charges that actually went through before the fraud alert was triggered.

A " fraud investigation '' was initiated, which consisted of a single phone call to me by a rep from XXXX, who asked me 3 questions ( 1. Did I still have the card. 2. did I lend anybody the card. 3. did I deal with the vendors in question or receive any goods or services from them ). After that phone call, the charges were marked as non-fraud and billed to me. I was not notified of this " resolution '', and only when I received a bill ( my card balance was XXXX at the time, as I hadn't even used the card for several months before the fraud took place ). I called back and re-disputed the charges and sent in a copy of my timecard from work showing that I was on the other side of the state and unable to make any charges. Two weeks later, I discovered the dispute was again closed as non-fraud, and again, I was not notified of this " resolution ''. I had to call in for an update to find out. I am now in the process of trying to obtain security video footage from my job to prove I was on the other side of the state, because I don't know what else I can do to prove to these people that I did not make these charges.

My credit score is currently XXXX. I refuse to let this terrible company ruin my credit because they refuse to credit my account for these fraudulent charges that the credit card agreement I signed states I have {$0.00} liability for. 
I will file every complaint, make every police report, and call their fraud department until I die if that's what it takes. I will upload my timecard here as well.
